behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (failure boundary): This behavior depends on real watcher recursion behavior across directory boundaries, which is an OS/filesystem event boundary, not just local logic. Rationale 2 (mock-hide): Injecting fake events can pass even if real recursive watching is misconfigured or unsupported on the host. Integration-brake check: (a) yes, needs real OS filesystem events -> System; (b) no AI output; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM stage handoff. Rejection-gate: Higher levels rejected because behavior text contains no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temporary watched root, create a nested subdirectory, write a file inside it, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (failure boundary): The behavior text targets dedup decision logic for same path plus time-window comparison, which is a local algorithm/data-structure boundary. Rationale 2 (mock-hide): No real OS/network/subprocess is required to expose wrong window arithmetic or keying by path. Integration-brake check: (a) no real infra required; (b) no AI output; (c) no agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require watcher/queue/module handoff; it explicitly scopes to dedup logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call dedup component twice with identical path and controlled clock/configured window; assert first request is accepted and second is rejected within window, then accepted after window expiry.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (failure boundary): The trigger condition is explicitly OS-emitted duplicate events, which is a real filesystem event behavior boundary. Rationale 2 (mock-hide): Synthetic single-event or idealized duplicate-event mocks can miss real event burst patterns/timing that cause double processing. Integration-brake check: (a) yes, real OS event behavior -> System; (b) no AI output; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection-gate: Higher levels rejected because behavior text has no AI/ML model decision or agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watched directory, perform one write operation known to generate multiple filesystem notifications on the platform, and assert one and only one output artifact is produced.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (failure boundary): Directory deletion/recreation changes underlying OS watch handles/inodes; this is a real filesystem lifecycle boundary. Rationale 2 (mock-hide): In-process fakes usually do not model watch invalidation/rebind behavior after directory replacement. Integration-brake check: (a) yes, real OS directory/watch semantics -> System; (b) no AI output; (c) no agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection-gate: Higher levels rejected because no AI/ML or multi-agent steps are present in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, delete watched directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed, proving watch recovery without daemon restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (failure boundary): Correctness depends on real file metadata (creation timestamp) and concurrent arrival ordering under actual filesystem timing/granularity. Rationale 2 (mock-hide): Mock timestamps/events can deterministically enforce order and hide platform-specific timestamp precision/race issues. Integration-brake check: (a) yes, real filesystem metadata/event timing is part of failure mode -> System; (b) no AI output; (c) no agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection-gate: Higher levels rejected because behavior text includes no AI/ML judgment or multi-agent workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Create several files near-simultaneously in the real watched tree with controlled creation times, then assert output processing order matches creation timestamp ordering observed from filesystem metadata.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (failure boundary): This is a real-time filesystem race between write completion and event handling, which depends on OS write/flush semantics. Rationale 2 (mock-hide): Stubbed file events and instantaneous writes hide partial-write windows and lock/flush timing failures. Integration-brake check: (a) yes, real OS/filesystem behavior required -> System; (b) no AI output; (c) no agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection-gate: Higher levels rejected because behavior text has no model-output correctness or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Start a slow/streaming write to a file in the watched directory, ensure arrival event occurs before completion, and assert daemon delays processing until writer closes and full content is available.
